stem: A healthcare study measures average patient recovery rate across 3 hospitals, each containing multiple departments. hospital A has 8 observations with mean 53.0 % (within-hospital SD = 5.3). hospital B has 20 observations with mean 61.6 % (within-hospital SD = 8.4). hospital C has 5 observations with mean 56.4 % (within-hospital SD = 3.8). The between-hospital standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 3.9 and 9.8 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average patient recovery rate for hospital C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-hospital variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for hospital C: 56.5 % (shrunk from raw 56.4). Range [56.5, 56.8] as between-hospital SD varies from 3.9 to 9.8. Pooled mean: 58.7.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 58.7 % (weighted average of 3 hospital means).
Shrinkage factor for hospital C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/5). At σ_between=6.5: B = 0.936.
Interval [56.5, 56.8] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (56.4) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (58.7) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
